subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequence of introduction, modelling, analysis and conclusion supporting logical flow
Thorough table of contents and consistent headings give sound organizational clarity
Precise, well-stated aim that guides the exploration
weaknesses: Duplicated table labels (e.g., âTable 5â appears twice) causing occasional confusion
Repetition of definitions and raw URLs in the body reduce conciseness
Minor typographical errors and inconsistent numbering slightly disrupt the structure
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band assessments indicate coherent and logical development (Band 3), sound organization (Band 3) but only partial conciseness (Band 2). The work is well structured and easy to navigate but not exceptional due to verbosity and labelling lapses, warranting a mid-band score of 3 out of 4.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, scatter plots, Lorenz curves) to aid understanding
Logical interweaving of working steps, results and commentary for clear traceability
Consistent definition and use of variables and notation throughout most calculations
weaknesses: Axes on several figures are faint or cropped, reducing clarity
Minor typographical errors (e.g. â0.0.29987â) and inconsistent notation in formulas
Lack of error bars or additional statistical visuals limits sophistication
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although strand assessments rated Mathematical Communication at Band 3 across notation, representations and structure, the student consistently employs a variety of effective representations and maintains logical clarity throughout. Minor technical issues prevent a perfect score, but the overall quality justifies elevating this to 4 out of 4.

strengths: Significant independent thinking in sourcing World Bank data and applying quadratic regressions
Clear personal approach anchored by a relatable anecdote in the introduction
Good initiative in performing manual Pearson calculations
weaknesses: Techniques remain standard and closely follow textbook procedures
Mathematical pathway is conventional, limiting innovation
Creative extensions beyond syllabus content are minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands received Band 2 for independent thinking, personal approach and creativity. The exploration shows a clear personal engagement but lacks the originality and innovation needed for the top band, so a score of 2 out of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on trends, policy influences and data limitations
Thoughtful analysis of results in economic context
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggested future extensions
weaknesses: Reflection remains descriptive rather than deeply critical or quantitative
No discussion of model fit metrics (e.g., RÂ²) or statistical significance
Evaluation of implications lacks synthesis and depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each reflection strand was assessed at Band 2: meaningful reflection, thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation. While the student engages thoughtfully, the depth and critical insight required for Band 3 are not fully present. A score of 2 out of 3 reflects this balanced performance.

strengths: Expert and comprehensive use of relevant mathematics (integration, Lorenz curves, quadratic regression, Pearson correlation) directly addressing the aim
Mathematics is fully commensurate with SL level and expertly applied to real-world data
Thorough understanding demonstrated through correct set-up and interpretation of results
weaknesses: Minor rounding inconsistencies (e.g., 0.15483 vs 0.1547) and small numeric errors
Occasional mismatches between text descriptions and tabulated values
Some notation clarifications (e.g., explicit A and B definitions) could strengthen accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed Use of Mathematics at Band 4 across relevance, level appropriateness and accuracy, but the studentâs consistent, expert application of multiple advanced techniques and deep alignment with the research question merits elevation to the top band. Minor slips do not undermine the overall expertise, justifying a full 6 out of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Working steps, results and verbal commentary are logically interwoven, so the reader can trace the reasoning. Occasional typographical errors and a few dense paragraphs limit ultimate clarity, but the overall **logical structure is good and communication is clear.**

Derivatives are not required, and integrations and r-value calculations are mostly accurate (minor rounding slips only). Explanations demonstrate **good understanding** of both economic meaning and mathematical process, aligning with âmostly correctâ performance without the flawless precision required for bands 5â6.

The student employs **tables, scatter plots, Lorenz curves and equations** to convey information. Representations are appropriate and aid understanding; however, axes are sometimes faint or cropped and no error bars / further statistical visuals are added, so the use is effective rather than highly sophisticated.

The report follows a clear sequence â introduction, mathematical modelling, analysis, conclusion â and the argument generally develops in a logical order. Transitions between Indonesia and UK calculations are smooth and the reader can follow how each mathematical outcome feeds the narrative. Minor lapses (e.g. duplicated explanations, small typographical errors in formulas) prevent the work from feeling âexcellentâ, but overall **the development is coherent and logical** rather than merely âsomewhat coherent.â

Using quadratic best-fit curves and manual r-value tables shows **some initiative**, but there is little novel modelling or creative extension beyond syllabus techniques, keeping the strand at a âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ level.

Results are interpreted against economic context and correlation strength, showing **thoughtful analysis**. However, statistical uncertainty, model validity and potential alternative methods are only briefly touched, preventing deeper insight.

The study states limitations (data gaps, small country sample) and offers future extensions, giving a **clear evaluation of outcomes and implications**. Discussion remains broad; there is no quantitative error check, so evaluation is adequate but not comprehensive.

The student reflects on trends, policy influences and limitations of data coverage, demonstrating **meaningful reflection** throughout. Reflection is descriptive rather than deeply critical, so it does not reach the top band.

Variables are defined, integral notation is mostly correct and symbols such as A, B, G are used consistently. Minor slips (e.g. â0.0.29987â) and informal wording in places show that accuracy is *mostly* but not *fully* consistent.

Personal motivation is stated in the introduction and the student frames reflections around Indonesian experiences, displaying a **clear personal approach**. Yet the mathematical pathway (integrate â compute Gini â correlate) is conventional, limiting innovativeness.

Most material is relevant to the aim, but explanations of basic concepts (e.g. repeated definitions of Lorenz curves and Gini coefficient) and long verbatim quotations add length without new insight. Consequently **conciseness is only partial**; the exploration is more verbose than necessary for the mathematics presented.

Integration, Lorenz modelling and Pearson correlation **directly address the stated aims**. All mathematics employed is relevant and each technique contributes to answering the research question, meeting the âeffectiveâ descriptor.

The choice to model *self-sourced* World Bank data with quadratic regressions and to perform manual Pearson calculations shows **significant independent thought**, but techniques are standard and closely follow textbook procedure, so the evidence is not âoutstanding.â

A structured table of contents, numbered sections, and consistent headings give the exploration **sound organisational clarity**. Introduction, method, results, limitations and references are easy to locate. However, the appendix pages and a few tables/figures are mis-labelled (e.g. âTable 5â appears twice) and there is occasional repetition across sections, so it does not reach the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard.

Quadratic regression, definite integration and statistical correlation are solidly within SL level and are **fully commensurate with course expectations**. The student applies them correctly to real data, but the work does not move beyond standard syllabus content, so a higher band is not justified.

annotations.comment: The limitation section notes methodological gaps but omits comment on model fit quality (e.g., RÂ²). Include such metrics to strengthen the evaluation.
The concluding paragraphs reiterate earlier points but do not synthesize implications. Add a concise summary linking results back to the global development context.
You note Gini values from quadratic fits but do not quantify fit error. Including goodness-of-fit measures would deepen critical reflection on model validity.
In the B calculation you use 0.15483 but the table lists 0.1547. Align rounding consistently to avoid small numerical discrepancies.
The title clearly states the focus on comparing income inequality over 29 years, immediately orienting the reader and supporting coherent development.
The table of contents is thorough and reflects sound organization, guiding the reader to each section with clear page references.
Some table labels are duplicated (e.g., âTable 5â appears twice). Ensure each table/figure has a unique identifier to avoid reader confusion.
The personal anecdote in the introduction effectively anchors the exploration in real experience, demonstrating clear personal engagement.
Raw URLs in the body disrupt flow. Move full URLs to footnotes or the reference list, using in-text citations to enhance conciseness and readability.
The same source (Macrotrends) is cited twice consecutively. Consolidate these footnotes or remove duplicates to maintain conciseness.
The stated aim is precise and outlines both mathematical modelling and correlation analysis, effectively guiding the exploration.
The bullet list of objectives repeats â1.â twice. Use consistent numbering (1, 2) to improve structural clarity.
Figure 1âs axes are faint and partly cropped; enhancing axis labels and line visibility would improve clarity in the Lorenz curve representation.
The description of the Gini formula uses informal wording (âThe area betweenâ¦â). Adopting precise mathematical notation throughout would strengthen communication.
Table 1 lists $G=\frac{B}{B+A}$ but the earlier formula used $G=\frac{B}{A}$. Ensure the notation is consistent across all definitions.
Using both tables and scatter plots to show distribution data and trendlines demonstrates effective use of multiple representations.
When introducing the definite integral, explicitly reference the notation for A and B to reinforce clarity of the mathematical process.
The inline calculation shows âA = 0.0.29987 + â¦â which contains a typo. Correct coefficient notation to maintain accuracy.
In the first integral evaluation you wrote 0.299 instead of 0.29987 in the constant term. Verify coefficients consistently when substituting bounds.
The UKâs 1984 Lorenz table shows 99.90% rather than 100% at the last quintile, yet Indonesiaâs is exactly 100%. Check original data and rounding conventions.
Axes on Figures 2 and 3 lack explicit labels for population share and income share. Labeling both axes will improve clarity.
The Pearson interpretation table is inserted without integration in text. Discuss its use and thresholds explicitly when interpreting your correlation results.
The conclusion text states the UK Gini rose to 0.3983, but your table shows 0.3083. This numeric inconsistency needs correction.
When discussing the Indonesia scatter plot, link specific fluctuations to data points or years on the plot to strengthen the analysis.
The text describes a decline in the UK Gini trend, but Figure 6âs best-fit line shows a slight upward slope into the 2000s. Reconcile this description with the graph.
The Pearson formula layout mixes inline text and bullet descriptions, making it dense. Consider separating symbol definitions into a list for clarity.
The r = 0.7052 result is described but not classified. According to your table, this falls in the âStrongâ range; explicitly state this.
The narrative omits discussion of statistical significance or confidence in the correlation. Address p-values or error bounds for a more critical analysis.
